pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/2300) - Acrolein NFPA 704 rating explicitly states Health=4 'Can be lethal'
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/2300) - Acrolein NFPA 704 rating explicitly states Flammability=3
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/2300) - Acrolein NFPA 704 rating explicitly states Instability=3
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/2300) - No special hazard notation listed

Conflicting sources: (1) Web search indicates Health=3 from multiple SDS references; (2) Another source indicates Health=1; (3) CAMEO Chemicals states 'NFPA 704: data unavailable' for both solid and aqueous solution. Metadata value of 2 does not match any authoritative source found.
Multiple sources confirm Sodium Chlorate Flammability=0 (not itself flammable, though it is a strong oxidizer)
Multiple sources confirm Sodium Chlorate Instability=1 (reacts explosively with organic matter and some metals)
Multiple sources confirm Sodium Chlorate carries OX (Oxidizer) special hazard designation
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/4654) - Toluene NFPA 704 rating explicitly states Health=2 'Can cause temporary incapacitation or residual injury'
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/4654) - Toluene NFPA 704 rating explicitly states Flammability=3, flash point 40°F
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/4654) - Toluene NFPA 704 rating explicitly states Instability=0 'Normally stable, even under fire conditions'
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/4654) - No special hazard notation listed
Calculated as maximum of verified values: max(4, ?, 2) where ? = disputed Sodium Chlorate value (1, 2, or 3). Result is 4 regardless, matching metadata.
Calculated as maximum of verified values: max(3, 0, 3) = 3
Calculated as maximum of verified values: max(3, 1, 0) = 3. Depends on Sodium Chlorate Instability=1 being correct (verified).
OX is the only special hazard among the three chemicals, all others are NONE
Setup scripts confirm Desktop directory creation at this location (setup_task.sh line 16, export_result.sh line 10)

privileged_info_summary: The task sources real chemical safety data from CAMEO Chemicals (cameochemicals.noaa.gov). Verified ground truth values: Acrolein (H:4, F:3, I:3, Special:NONE), Toluene (H:2, F:3, I:0, Special:NONE), and area maximum ratings (H:4, F:3, I:3, Special:OX) are all accurate per CAMEO Chemicals official NFPA 704 ratings. However, Sodium Chlorate metadata claims Health=2, but authoritative sources indicate Health is either 1 or 3, with CAMEO reporting NFPA 704 data unavailable. All other Sodium Chlorate values (Flammability=0, Instability=1, Special=OX) are verified correct.
